data.title: Renowned chef's exit sparks discussions among chefs on evolving restaurant kitchens and practices.
data.description: Renowned chef René Redzepi's impact on the industry has sparked a discussion among fellow chefs about the extent to which restaurant kitchens can evolve and the degree of transformation needed. The debate centers on exploring new approaches and strategies, with a focus on incorporating innovative techniques into kitchen operations. Reevaluating traditional norms and embracing change are key themes as chefs consider adapting to evolving culinary trends and consumer preferences.

data.title: Domestic Metals Completes Surface IP Geophysical Survey at Smart Creek and Announces Amendment to Warrant Terms of Unit Private Placement
data.description: A recently conducted surface IP geophysical survey by Domestic Metals at Smart Creek has been successfully completed. The survey results are expected to aid in better understanding the area's geology and potential resources. Concurrently, Domestic Metals has made an announcement regarding an amendment to the warrant terms of its Unit Private Placement. This amendment is likely to impact the terms and conditions associated with the private placement opportunities offered by the company. The endeavor signifies a strategic move by Domestic Metals to potentially enhance its financial standing and investment attractiveness. This development comes as part of the company's efforts to leverage opportunities in a evolving market scenario.

data.title: Silvercrest Asset Management (SAMG) to Announce Fourth Quarter and Year-End 2025 Results and Host Investor Conference Call
data.description: On March 13, 2026, Silvercrest Asset Management Group Inc. (NASDAQ: SAMG) revealed plans to conduct a teleconference at 8:30 am Eastern Time on March 17, 2026. This conference aims to deliberate on the financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and full year concluded on December 31, 2025. Ahead of the U.S. equity markets' opening, a press release disclosing the results will be distributed and can be accessed on http://ir.silvercrestgroup.com/.

data.title: NASDAQ: MCW Investigation Notice: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP is Investigating Proposed Take Private Transaction and Encourages Mister Car Wash, Inc. (NASDAQ: MCW) Investors to Contact the Firm
data.description: A prominent law firm, Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP, recently initiated a probe into Mister Car Wash, Inc. The investigation, concerning the actions of the company's board of directors and major stockholder Leonard Green, is aimed at scrutinizing their activities. Mister Car Wash, Inc., listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol MCW, is the focal point of this inquiry. For further updates, visit the law firm's website at www.ktmc.com.

data.title: CIEE BridgeUSA Celebration Tour Visits Park City to Highlight How BridgeUSA Makes America Safer, Stronger, and More Prosperous
data.description: In its ninth stop on a nationwide roadshow, the Council on International Educational Exchange (CIEE) honored outstanding host employers and supporters of global exchange in Utah at Park City on March 13, 2026. The event celebrated the contributions of key stakeholders to international education.

data.title: ASEAN Records and ASIA Records Recognise 34 New Records
data.description: A deputy minister recently attended the launch of the 8th ASEAN Records and 2nd ASIA Records Gather of Achievers event in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia on March 13, 2026. The event, which celebrated the Chinese New Year in 2026, showcased achievements in ASEAN and ASIA. This gathering marked an important occasion where notable records were recognized in the region.

data.title: Pete Hegseth: Shipping lane issues are being addressed. 2,500 Marines deployed to Middle East to strengthen efforts.
data.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth acknowledged that the recent disturbances in the Strait of Hormuz are a current concern that they are addressing. Approximately 2,500 Marines are being deployed to the Middle East to enhance military operations in the region.

data.title: Judge dismisses Justice Dept.'s subpoenas of Federal Reserve, hindering probe of Trump's opponents. Boasberg criticizes motive.
data.description: Judge James E. Boasberg criticized the Washington U.S. attorney’s office for pursuing a case against Jerome H. Powell, suggesting it was influenced by President Trump’s personal motives.
